framework.name: OWASP Agentic AI Vulnerability Scoring System (AIVSS)
framework.description: Combines base vulnerability severity with agentic amplification factors into a 0–10 composite score. Use these dimensions to assess and govern agentic AI systems; pair with OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications (2025) for application-layer threats.

controls.title: Agent autonomy & execution scope
controls.description: Degree of unsupervised or asynchronous action the agent may take without human approval. Higher autonomy increases blast radius and maps to AIVSS amplification.
controls.implementationGuidance: Define autonomy levels (L0–L5); require approval gates for high-impact actions; cap concurrent autonomous steps; document kill switches.
controls.evidenceGuidance: Autonomy policy, approval routing configs, audit logs of autonomous runs, escalation playbooks.

controls.title: Tool, plugin, and API scope
controls.description: Breadth and privilege of tools, MCP servers, plugins, and APIs the agent can invoke. Excessive scope increases exploitability.
controls.implementationGuidance: Least-privilege tool allowlists; separate read vs write tools; OAuth scopes; sandbox dangerous operations.
controls.evidenceGuidance: Tool inventory, permission matrix, security reviews for new tools, integration tests.

controls.title: Memory, state & long-term persistence
controls.description: Whether the agent retains state across sessions (memory, vector stores, scratchpads). Persistent state increases data exfiltration and prompt-injection persistence.
controls.implementationGuidance: TTL and retention for agent memory; encrypt stored trajectories; isolate per-tenant memory; periodic purge.
controls.evidenceGuidance: Memory architecture docs, retention policies, encryption at rest, access reviews.

controls.title: Delegation & multi-agent orchestration
controls.description: Use of sub-agents, delegation chains, or planner–executor patterns that increase indirect paths to harmful outcomes.
controls.implementationGuidance: Limit delegation depth; identity per sub-agent; propagate policy and budgets; log full orchestration traces.
controls.evidenceGuidance: Orchestration diagrams, trace samples, policy inheritance rules, max-depth settings.

controls.title: External connectivity & network exposure
controls.description: Internet, arbitrary URLs, or third-party APIs reachable by the agent. Expands attack surface for prompt injection and SSRF-style abuse.
controls.implementationGuidance: Egress allowlists; URL filtering; no raw fetch to internal networks; egress proxy with logging.
controls.evidenceGuidance: Network diagrams, firewall rules, egress logs, penetration test results.

controls.title: Identity, authorization & trust boundaries
controls.description: How the agent is authenticated to downstream systems and whether it can impersonate users or escalate privileges.
controls.implementationGuidance: Dedicated service principals for agents; no long-lived user tokens; OAuth device flow where appropriate; RBAC on agent actions.
controls.evidenceGuidance: IAM design, token lifetime policies, RBAC matrices, periodic access reviews.

controls.title: Sensitive data in agent context
controls.description: PII, secrets, or regulated data present in prompts, memory, or tool outputs. Amplifies impact of disclosure or misuse.
controls.implementationGuidance: Data classification; redaction before model context; DLP on tool I/O; minimize retention.
controls.evidenceGuidance: Classification schema, DLP rules, redaction logs, DPIA where applicable.

controls.title: Base vulnerability severity
controls.description: Underlying technical weakness (aligned with CWE/CVE or internal findings) before agentic amplification. Feeds the base score in AIVSS.
controls.implementationGuidance: Triage with CVSS or equivalent; link findings to components; track fixes and regressions.
controls.evidenceGuidance: Vulnerability tickets, CVSS scores, patch evidence, retest results.

controls.title: Scoring methodology & review cadence
controls.description: Governance for computing, documenting, and updating AIVSS scores (0–10); calibration across teams; periodic reassessment as agents evolve.
controls.implementationGuidance: Publish internal AIVSS calculator or worksheet; version methodology; review at release and major capability changes.
controls.evidenceGuidance: Methodology doc, score history, review meeting notes, change management records.
